content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
William H. Ihrke 
La Quinta City Attorney 
Rutan & Tucker, LLP 
61 l Anton Blvd., Suite 1400 
Costa Mesa, CA 92626 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-092 
Dear Mr. Ihrke: 
June 7, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of La Quinta Mayor Pro Tern John 
Pena regarding his duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Refonn Act (the 
"Act").1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section I 090. 
QUESTION 
May Mayor Pro Tern Pena participate in decisions regarding the "Villas at Old Town" 
project despite the fact that his residence is located roughly 750 feet from the proposed location of 
the proposed development? 
CONCLUSION 
Based on the magnitude of the development that is the subject of the governmental decision 
in relation to that property's current use, the Mayor Pro Tern would have a conflict of interest. 
FACTS 
The City is negotiating with Marvin Investments, Inc. ("Investor") regarding the price and 
terms of payment for modifications related to an agreement for the purchase, sale and development 
of the real property on which the Villas at Old Town project would be located (the "Agreement"). 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No, A-16-092 
Page No. 2 
The site is approximately 3 acres of undeveloped land located in the "Lower Cove" area of La 
Quinta, on the comer of Calle Tampico and Desert Club Drive. The primary access and arterial 
roadway to the undeveloped real property (and proposed Villas at Old Town project) is (and would 
be) Calle Tampico. Specifically: 
• Abutting the project site to the north is Calle Tampico. 
• To the west are existing parking lots, and situated across Desert Club Drive is Old Town La 
Quinta. 
• To the south is an existing utility company facility and Avenida La Fonda. 
• To the east is the La Quinta Civic Center Campus with an existing palm tree grove, public 
parking lot, and the La Quinta Library and Wellness Center immediately adjacent. 
The Lower Cove area is generally flat, but views of the local Santa Rosa and San Jacinto 
Mountains are available from virtually any unobstructed street level vantage point. The 
undeveloped real property is located at the corner of Calle Tampico and Desert Club Drive. The 
primary access and arterial roadway to the undeveloped real property (and proposed Villas at Old 
Town project) is (and would be) Calle Tampico. 
The Agreement is intended to enable Investor to purchase the land and develop a mixed use 
development of approximately 24,000 square feet of first floor retail space located in multiple 
buildings, 72 multi-family residential units located above those retail spaces, surface level parking 
lots, and a 60-unit multi-family residential building with underground property. The entire project is 
to be completed in two phases. The height of the project is not anticipated to exceed two or three 
stories, and upon completion, the project is anticipated to be similar to the height and general design 
of the "Old Town La Quinta" shops and restaurants already developed and located across the street 
on Desert Club Drive. 
Mayor Pro Tern Pena owns a single-family home, which serves as his personal residence, 
located in the Lower Cove area of La Quinta. Mayor Pro Tern Pena' s residence is located to the 
southeast, approximately 750 feet "as the crow flies" from that outer-most point of undeveloped 
land for the proposed project. 
• Mayor Pro Tern Pena's residence is typical of the single family subdivided homes in the 
Lower Cove neighborhood, which are generally one-story "mid-century modern" single 
family residences on lots that average approximately 3,000-6,000 square feet. 
• The Mayor Pro Tern's residence is surrounded by other single-family homes to the north, 
west, and south, and has driveway access from the east (i.e., the residence is on the west side 
of the residential street). 
• The residence is not situated on an "elevated lot" and does not have a view of the 
undeveloped land where the Villas at Old Town project is proposed. 


File No. A-16-092 
Page No. 3 
• The street on which the Mayor Pro Tern's residence is located is a public street, but it is a 
local access/neighborhood street and not designated for commercial vehicles and does not 
serve as a major or minor arterial roadway servicing commuter traffic. 
• The nearest arterial roadway servicing commercial vehicles and commuter ("through") 
traffic is Calle Tampico, which is approximately 1,000 feet away from the Mayor Pro Tern's 
residence, but the street on which he Mayor Pro Tern's residence is located is not accessiqle 
from Calle Tampico. 
• The street on which his residence is located is separated by a "buffer zone" resulting from 
the City's civic center park and library facility located between Calle Tampico and Avenida 
La Fonda. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any state or local public official from making, participating in 
making, or using his or her official position to influence a government decision in which the official 
has an interest specified in Section 87103. A public official has a financial interest in a government 
decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a 
material financial effect on one or more of the public official's interests, including an interest in real 
property. (Section 87103(b).) 
Foreseeability and Materiality 
Foreseeability: Regulation 18701(b), as applied to your facts, provides that a financial effect 
need not be likely to be considered reasonably foreseeable. In general, if the financial effect can be 
recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is reasonably 
foreseeable. If the financial result cannot be expected absent extraordinary circumstances not 
subject to the public official's control, it is not reasonably foreseeable.2 
Real Property Materiality: Many factors apply to determine whether a decision could have a 
material financial effect on real property. (See Regulation 18702.2.) As applies here, the financial 
effect will be material if the decision: 
• Would change the character of the parcel of real property by substantially altering traffic 
levels or intensity of use, including parking, of property surrounding the official's real 
property parcel, the view, privacy, noise levels, or air quality, including odors, or any other 
factors that would affect the market value of the real property parcel in which the official 
has a financial interest. _(Regulation l 8702(a)(l 0).) 
• Would cause a reasonably prudent person, using due care and consideration under the 
circumstances, to believe that the governmental decision was of such a nature that its 
reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the market value of the official's property. 
(Regulation 18702(a)(12).) 
2 There is a different test where an interest is explicitly involved in a decision, which docs not apply here. 
(Regulation l 870l(a).) 


File No. A-16-092 
Page No. 4 
Based on the magnitude of the development that is the subject of the governmental decision 
in relation to that property's current use, the Mayor Pro Tern would have a conflict of interest even 
though his property is beyond 500 feet of site. 
The project site consists of three acres of currently vacant and undeveloped property. Once 
complete, the site will be developed with a mixed-use development of approximately 24,000 square 
feet of first floor retail space located in multiple buildings, 72 multi-family residential units located 
above those retail spaces, surface level parking lots, and a 60-unit multi-family residential building 
with underground property. This significant change the character of the parcel could substantially 
alter traffic levels and could affect the market value of the real property in the area. (Regulation 
18702(a)(l0).) Alternatively, the conversion of the vacant lot to additional retail and residences 
could enhance the marketability of homes in the area. 
As noted above, reasonable foreseeability does not require certainty or mathematical 
precision. In general, if the financial effect can be recognized as a realistic possibility {as here) and 
more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is reasonably foreseeable. Thus, consistent with Regulation 
l 8702{a)(l 2), the Mayor Pro Tern's participation in these decisions could reasonably lead to at least 
the perception that his decision-making could be compromised by financial advantages and 
disadvantages to his property. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (9 I 6) 322-5660. 
JWW:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel

the proposed development?

project despite the fact that his residence is located roughly 750 feet from the proposed location of 
the proposed development?

CONCLUSION: Based on the magnitude of the development that is the subject of the governmental decision 
in relation to that property's current use, the Mayor Pro Tern would have a conflict of interest.
